583520.title: VARIODYN ONE Digital Call Station DCS plus / DKM plus
583520.description: The VARIODYN ONE Digital Call Station DCS plus allows selection of loudspeaker circuits, transmission of voice announcements, gongs, and alarms. It connects via standard CAT5 cable to the VARIODYN ONE INC-D control device, supporting up to four simultaneous audio signals. Connection can be redundant, up to 300m via CAT5 or 20km via fibre-optic cabling. The DCS plus can be expanded with up to six DKM plus digital keyboard modules (PN 583526), providing up to 120 keys and LEDs per station. The microphone and cable connection are permanently monitored. It includes an external audio input/output for connecting devices like CD players. Features include 12 freely configurable keys with independent programmable LEDs, a built-in loudspeaker, and an electret gooseneck microphone. It is designed for high failure safety and cost-effective CAT5 cabling, and can be fitted into a desk surface.

LCD.title: LCD80 Mimic Annunciator
LCD.description: The Honeywell LCD-80 is an 80-character, backlit Liquid Crystal Display (LCD) annunciator designed for use with Honeywell BC200 fire indicator panels (FIP). Up to 32 LCD-80 units can be connected onto a two-wire distributed ring. It operates in two primary modes: "Terminal" and "Annunciator" mode. Common features include an 80-character backlit LCD (20 characters x 4 lines), time and date display, and the ability to be remotely located up to 1000 metres from the control panel. It features a mutable local piezo sounder with alarm/fault/isolate resound and offers flush or surface mount options. The device utilizes an EIA-485 interface, supporting up to 32 units per FIP. In Terminal mode, the LCD-80 mimics the BC200 display, showing time, date, events, device type identifiers, and alarm/fault/isolate counts, with individual and scroll buttons for events and a mute function for the internal sounder. In Annunciator mode, it functions as a remote alphanumeric display module, showing up to 64 pre-programmed messages (60 characters each) activated by script events in the FIP, with scroll functionality for viewing multiple active messages.

600GB-SAS3.5.title: Fulfillment for Contract Logistics
600GB-SAS3.5.description: Warehouse Star is a cloud-based Warehouse Management System (WMS) and Fulfillment solution designed by Bitergo GmbH (owned by DB Schenker AG) to help businesses ramp up their warehouses quickly and optimize logistics processes. It focuses on Warehousing, B2B, SaaS, and SCM, serving over 100 customers, handling 25,000 picks per day, and processing 1 million EDI transactions per month. Key benefits include a 50% faster ramp-up (simple warehouses in 2 days worldwide), a 40% increase in picking efficiency, fast onboarding of workers, easy connectivity to web shops and ERP systems, and on-demand pricing for seasonal peaks. Core functionalities cover lot management, serial number support, expiration dates, cross-dock movements, multi-tenant support, load support, value-added services, item-specific packaging, master data import via Excel, and open interfaces via REST-API. Add-ons include advanced reporting, AS/RS support, SAP + EDI connection, Forklift and AGV control, vendor-managed inventory, and customs/bonded goods. The system optimizes goods in and storage, picking, inventory, and shipping processes. It is compatible with Android-based mobile apps and selected hardware from leading scanner manufacturers like Zebra, Honeywell, Sato, Gigaset, and Nimmsta. A 30-day freemium trial is available.

A133230-00.title: COMMANDER Shotblasting Helmet
A133230-00.description: The Honeywell COMMANDER is a series of shotblasting helmets with air supply, offering proven solutions for users in harsh environments. These helmets provide high-level protection with comfortable pressure, featuring a rubber coating to reduce noise and wear. Key features include a low flow indicator for increased safety, shoulder, back, and chest protection via a cape, a disposable visor protecting the main visor, a mesh screen, and an adjustable headband for comfort. All parts are easily replaceable. The cape is available in three versions: leather, cotton, or waterproof bisonyl for chemical hazards. The helmet is polyester reinforced with fiberglass and has a rubber coating. It offers a focused field of view of 145mmx90mm with no visual distraction. Other available product numbers include A133230-00 (with bisonyl cape), A133730-00 (with cotton cape), and A133230-01 (with bisonyl cape).

CATALIST.title: UOP R-264 CCR Platforming™ Catalyst
CATALIST.description: UOP's R-264 catalyst is a low platinum and high density spherical catalyst designed for use in continuous regeneration reforming units (such as the CCR Platforming™ process) for upgrading low-quality naphtha. It converts naphtha, in the presence of hydrogen, into high-octane reformate, hydrogen, motor fuel blending stocks for gasoline, BTX aromatics, and high-purity hydrogen for other refinery processes. R-264 provides maximum operational flexibility, allowing for increased throughput or higher yields through two modes of operation: high-activity or high-yield. Key benefits include excellent C5+ yields for motor fuel applications, excellent aromatics yields for BTX applications, excellent hydrogen yields, excellent activity to maintain required feed rate and octane target, low coke make from optimized metals content and unique base formulation, high yield stability over life of catalyst, and long catalyst life from high strength base and high chloride retention. It can be supplied in oxidized or reduced form, with reduced catalyst providing faster start-up and on-spec production. A higher platinum version is also available. Since its commercialization in 2004, more than 70 loads have been sold.

COLOR.title: Honeywell Industrial Safety Branding Guidelines
COLOR.description: This document outlines the branding guidelines for Honeywell Industrial Safety (HIS), a global provider of personal protective equipment (PPE) and gas detection devices. It details how distributors should consistently position and present Honeywell brands, logos, color palettes, and type-fonts. The guidelines cover the brand strategy, a five-tier hierarchy (Master, House, Endorsed, Standalone, Licensed Brands), logo usage rules (size, clear zone, backgrounds), trademark usage, wordmark elimination, legacy brand referencing, and best practices for promotional items and marketing material approval. Key color palette details include: Honeywell Red (#EE3124, R238 G49 B36, C9 M100 Y93 K0, PMS 1795), Honeywell Black (#000000, R0 G0 B0, C60 M40 Y40 K100, Munsell N2), Honeywell White (#FFFFFF, R255 G255 B255, C0 M0 Y0 K0, Munsell N9.5), Honeywell Grey 50 (#808080, R128 G128 B128, C0 M0 Y0 K50, PMS Cool Grey 8C, NCS S 5000-N), and Honeywell Grey 60 (#707070, R112 G112 B112, C0 M0 Y0 K56, PMS Cool Grey 9C, NCS S 6000-N).
